xEnrich 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Cell-type enrichment analysis using Tabula Sapiens scRNA data

Source: R/enrich_by_tabula.R
enrich_by_tabula.Rd

Applies enrichment testing across cell types within a single Tabula Sapiens organ dataset. Genes are filtered by minimum expression prevalence (PctExpr) before enrichment, reducing noise from genes detected in only a handful of cells. Expression values (MeanLogNorm) are used either directly as GSEA rankings or discretised for hypergeometric testing.

enrich_by_tabula(
  gene_list,
  reference_df,
  method = c("hypergeometric", "gsea"),
  cutoff_type = c("quantile", "threshold", "top_k", "specificity"),
  cutoff_value = 0.9,
  min_pct_expr = 0.1,
  universe = NULL,
  universe_type = c("global", "group", "provided"),
  min_overlap = 1L,
  min_background = 10L,
  gene_stats = NULL,
  alternative = c("greater", "less", "two.sided"),
  seed = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

gene_list

Character vector of gene symbols (the query set).

reference_df

Data frame of Tabula Sapiens expression data for one organ. Must contain columns GeneSymbol, CellType, MeanLogNorm, and PctExpr.

method

Character. "hypergeometric" (default) or "gsea".

cutoff_type

Character. How to define the expressed gene set per cell type for the hypergeometric test. Ignored when method = "gsea".

"quantile"

Genes at or above the cutoff_value quantile within the cell type (must be in (0, 1)). Default.

"threshold"

Genes with mean expression strictly above cutoff_value.

"top_k"

Top cutoff_value genes by mean expression (cutoff_value must be a positive integer).

"specificity"

Genes whose mean expression in the cell type is at least cutoff_value-fold above the mean across all other cell types in the organ. See the specificity section above.

cutoff_value

Numeric. Threshold interpreted per cutoff_type. Default 0.9 for quantile. Use 3 for specificity.

min_pct_expr

Numeric in [0, 1]. Minimum fraction of cells in a cell type that must express a gene for it to be included in the background. Default 0.1. Set to 0 to disable.

universe

Optional character vector. Required when universe_type = "provided".

universe_type

Character. Background gene universe construction:

"global"

All genes passing min_pct_expr across all cell types in reference_df.

"group"

Only genes passing min_pct_expr within the cell type being tested.

"provided"

Use universe directly.

min_overlap

Integer. Minimum overlap between gene_list and the cell-type universe. Default 1.

min_background

Integer. Minimum background size to attempt enrichment. Default 10.

gene_stats

Optional named numeric vector. Used only when method = "gsea" and universe_type = "provided".

alternative

Character. "greater" (default), "less", or "two.sided".

seed

Optional integer seed for GSEA permutations.

...

Additional named arguments passed to run_enrichment() for GSEA (n_perm, gsea_weight, adaptive, adaptive_mode, alpha, eps).

Value

A data frame with one row per cell type that passed filters, containing enrichment results and a padj column (BH adjustment across cell types). Returns an empty data frame if no cell types pass.

Details

This function is designed as the second step of the location-aware pipeline: after enrich_by_hpa_ihc() or enrich_by_hpa() identifies enriched organs, enrich_by_tabula() resolves which cell types within those organs drive the signal. Use run_location_pipeline() to run both steps automatically.

PctExpr filtering

PctExpr is the fraction of cells in a cell type that express a given gene (expression > 0). Genes below min_pct_expr are removed from the background for that cell type before enrichment. This prevents MeanLogNorm values inflated by a few outlier cells from entering the analysis. A value of 0.1 (10\ threshold in scRNA analysis.

cutoff_type = "specificity"

Unlike the other cutoff types, which rank genes by expression level within a single cell type, "specificity" computes a cross-cell-type fold change: a gene is included if its mean expression in the cell type is at least cutoff_value-fold above the mean across all other cell types in the same organ. This identifies genes that are distinctive to a cell type rather than merely highly expressed in it.

The distinction matters for interpretation: a quantile cutoff answers "which cell types express my query genes at high absolute levels?"; specificity answers "which cell types are my query genes distinctive to?" For most biomarker discovery questions, specificity is the more informative question.

This is conceptually equivalent to TissueEnrich's tissue-enriched gene definition applied at the cell-type level within a single organ, using the same cross-group fold-change approach as enrich_by_hpa(..., cutoff_type = "specificity").

Typical cutoff_value for cell-type specificity: 2 (moderate, includes co-expressed genes), 3 (default, selective), 5 (stringent, tissue-enriched equivalent). Lower than HPA tissue specificity because cell types within one organ share more genes than tissues across the whole body.

See also

run_location_pipeline(), enrich_by_hpa_ihc(), enrich_by_hpa()
